values: stable mimetic reality (no questioning of what is real)
single speculative layer but ontologically stable (e.g., one magical or SF premise taken as given)
ambiguous/unstable reality (reader encouraged to doubt whether events are real, dreamt, simulated, etc.)
multiple explicit reality layers or nested fictions (stories, simulations, or games within the world, with movement between levels)

features.q: How does the story position itself emotionally and critically toward the literary or mythic traditions it engages?
values: reverent/continuist (aims mainly to honour, preserve, or smoothly extend tradition)
critical/revisionist (explicitly reinterprets or challenges inherited stories)
parodic/irreverent (treats tradition primarily as material for jokes or send-up)
eclectic/agnostic (borrows elements without clear evaluative stance toward the sources)

features.q: What kinds of intertextual engagement with other works or traditions does the story employ?

values: Primarily explicit named references (titles, authors, specific works)
Primarily implicit echoes (genres, archetypes, unnamed myths)
Balanced mix of explicit and implicit

features.q: What kind of literary ambition does the story appear to signal in terms of market positioning and aesthetic aim?
values: primarily genre entertainment (plot- and trope-driven, minimal overt thematic or stylistic ambition)
crossover genre with literary aspirations (genre frame but clear stylistic/thematic ambition)
mainstream literary fiction (character and theme foregrounded over genre engines)
formally experimental/avant-garde (structure or language foregrounded over conventional readability)

values: legitimizing: authority is largely portrayed as justified, wise, or protective
ambivalent/mixed: authority has both protective and harmful aspects; critique and respect coexist
critical: authority is shown as corrupt, unjust, or harmful, with little endorsement
subversive: protagonists consciously work to undermine, escape, or overturn authority structures

features.q: Is there at least one character whose primary narrative function is to mediate between otherwise separate groups, classe
values: no: no clear bridging figure; groups relate directly or not at all

features.q: What best describes the main trajectory of the most central relationship or small cluster of relationships?

strengthening/repair: an existing strained or modest bond deepens or is healed
deterioration/betrayal: trust erodes, affection cools, or exploitation emerges
breakup/separation/death: a bond ends decisively through departure, rupture, or death
stable: the relationship's nature is largely unchanged by the story's end
cyclical/on-off: the relationship oscillates between connection and distance without net resolution
